arts.ttl: Bayern vs. Heidenheim preview: Banged-up league leaders in need of a strong statement
arts.sum: With their domestic cup hopes dashed early for the fifth consecutive year, Germany's record champions hope to solidify their place at the top of the Bundesliga table with a league win over struggling 1. FC Heidenheim.
Bayern Munich will be looking to extend their lead at the top of the Bundesliga table as they face Heidenheim at the Allianz Arena on Saturday. Vincent Kompany’s side remain…

arts.ttl: Bryan Zaragoza not likely to return to Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Bayern Munich recently loaned out Bryan Zaragoza, a young Spanish talent, to Osasuna FC after deciding that he would need more time to develop before being able to break through Bayern’s first 11. Awesome, then check out our Bavarian Podcast Works — Preview Show on Patreon, Spotify, or below:

arts.ttl: Daniel Peretz: Who is Bayern Munich's Israeli goalkeeper ready to face Heidenheim?
arts.sum: Daniel Peretz has had to bide his time since joining Bayern Munich from Maccabi Tel Aviv in 2023, but Israel's new main man is set to come into focus as he lines out for the Bavarian giants against Heidenheim this weekend. Peretz made his Bayern debut in the first round of the 2023/24 DFB Cup against Preußen Münster and kept a clean sheet in a 4-0 win while making three saves .

arts.ttl: Max Eberl admits Bayern Munich’s Mathys Tel “didn’t really grab his chance” against Borussia Dortmund
arts.sum: The idea that every player in Bayern Munich ’s squad has the same fair chance of making the starting XI is a little fanciful, especially when one considers the egos, dressing room influence and contracts at play, but Vincent Kompany has proven that he can change his mind if a squad player does well. Unfortunately, youngster Mathys Tel could not do the same when handed the chance to start against Borussia Dortmund .

arts.ttl: Weekend Warm-up: We might know the numbers for Bayern Munich to re-up Alphonso Davies...would you do it?; Bundesliga and Champions League predictions; Throwing it back to Pearl Jam; and MORE!
arts.sum: According to a report from Sport Bild journalist Christian Falk, it might take a total package of up to €95 million for Bayern Munich to re-sign Alphonso Davies on a five-year deal. Sky Sport journalist Florian Plettenberg says that there has been a lot of progress in talks between Bayern Munich and Alphonso Davies.

arts.ttl: Neuer and Gnabry ruled out of Bayern's clash with Heidenheim
arts.sum: Bayern Munich will be without Manuel Neuer and Serge Gnabry when they face Heidenheim on Saturday, Vincent Kompany has confirmed.

arts.ttl: Report: Bayern make "significant progress" in Davies contract talks
arts.sum: Sky Germany reports that Bayern Munich have made "significant progress" in their bid to extend Alphonso Davies' expiring contract.

arts.ttl: Premier League not entertained by Harry Kane’s suggestion the English top flight should have a winter break
arts.sum: Per the Daily Mail’s chief football reporter Sami Mokbel (via @iMiaSanMia ), Kane’s remarks about the English top flight and the lack of a winter, holiday break were essentially laughed off by officials from the league. Kane said he felt the break was something that really helped him personally last season and that is was a “shame” the Premier League does not have one.

arts.ttl: Breaking: Bayern Munich’s Manuel Neuer misses final training before Heidenheim, Sven Ulreich returns
arts.sum: According to a report from Bild (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ), Bayern Munich goalkeeper Manuel Neuer missed the final training session ahead of the team’s match against Heidenheim on Saturday: Manuel Neuer missed the final training session ahead of tomorrow’s game.
